name: trello
tagline: ClawHub (163★): Manage Trello boards, lists, and cards via the Trello REST API.
verdict: Failure handling is essentially absent: only rate limits get a mention, while auth errors, missing jq, invalid IDs, and API error responses are never addressed — a failed call just leaves you guessing. No version tracking, and no stated boundary against other project-management tools. Security is clean.

highlights.summary: The only failure-adjacent mention is stating the rate-limit numbers with no guidance on backoff/retry; auth errors, missing jq, invalid IDs, and API error responses are never addre
Create-card (and add-comment) are POST-create operations with no existence check; running the step twice creates duplicate cards/comments, and nothing in the skill mentions this.
No 'NOT for' / exclusion / out-of-scope text anywhere in frontmatter or body, and the skill covers enough surface (boards/lists/cards/comments) that misfire against adjacent PM-too
